target.title: Doctor: expose session lock findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes stale session write locks as structured doctor health findings.
- Adds dry-run repair effects for stale lock removal.
- Keeps the existing session-lock note/repair function authoritative for real normal `doctor` / `doctor --fix` behavior.
- Wires the structured check through the existing ordered doctor contribution path.

This is intentionally the session-lock slice from the doctor repair stack. It does not change session directory discovery, lock ownership detection, or the real mutating cleanup path.

## Stack

Stacked on #86627, current base head `bd1000b409d`. The only new session-lock commit in this PR is signed head `3fea011755`; the session-lock delta is 6 files.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project commands src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts` (7 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project commands src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts` (9 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts` (14 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project unit-fast src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts` (39 tests)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Stale session lock files are reported through `doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-locks`, and structured repair previews no longer claim that report-only live OpenClaw-owned locks would be removed. Locks whose stale reasons are only `too-old` / `hold-exceeded` now preview as preserved; removable stale locks still preview as removal.

Real environment tested:
WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04, source checkout `/root/src/openclaw-84366-proof` at signed head `650f6c09d4` (`fix(doctor): preserve report-only session locks in preview`). The CLI proof used an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and `HOME`. The preview-effect proof used a source harness importing `src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts` with injected owner-process args so the live OpenClaw-owned lock case is deterministic.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Created isolated temp state under `/tmp/openclaw-84366-proof-*` with `state/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl.lock` fixtures.
2. Ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/state/openclaw.json HOME=<temp>/home COREPACK_HOME=/root/.cache/node/corepack OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-locks`.
3. Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` source harness importing `detectStaleSessionLocks` and `sessionLockToRepairEffect` from `src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts` against the same isolated state.
4. Ran focused validation: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-session-locks.ts src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix:
CLI lint output returned nonzero as expected for warnings and reported one selected check:

```json
{
  "cliStatus": 1,
  "cliOk": false,
  "checksRun": 1,
  "checksSkipped": 22,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "core/doctor/session-locks",
      "severity": "warning",
      "message": "Stale session lock file: /tmp/openclaw-84366-proof-*/state/agents/main/sessions/bash-owner.jsonl.lock (non-openclaw-owner)",
      "fixHint": "Run \"openclaw doctor --fix\" to remove stale lock files automatically.",
      "pathBasename": "bash-owner.jsonl.lock"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "core/doctor/session-locks",
      "severity": "warning",
      "message": "Stale session lock file: /tmp/openclaw-84366-proof-*/state/agents/main/sessions/dead-owner.jsonl.lock (missing-pid)",
      "fixHint": "Run \"openclaw doctor --fix\" to remove stale lock files automatically.",
      "pathBasename": "dead-owner.jsonl.lock"
    }
  ]
}
```

Source preview-effect harness output showed the fixed distinction:

```json
{
  "summary": [
    {
      "file": "report-only.jsonl.lock",
      "staleReasons": ["too-old"],
      "effect": {
        "kind": "state",
        "action": "would-preserve-report-only-stale-session-lock",
        "dryRunSafe": false
      }
    },
    {
      "file": "dead-owner-harness.jsonl.lock",
      "staleReasons": ["missing-pid", "too-old"],
      "effect": {
        "kind": "state",
        "action": "would-remove-stale-session-lock",
        "dryRunSafe": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "reportOnlyStillExists": true,
  "removableStillExists": true
}
```

Focused validation passed: session-lock Vitest shard 14 tests passed; core-check Vitest shard 8 tests passed; targeted `oxlint` found 0 warnings/errors; `oxfmt --check` passed; `git diff --check` was clean.

Observed result after fix:
`doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/session-locks` surfaces structured stale-lock warnings from the isolated source CLI run. The patched repair-effect path preserves report-only live OpenClaw-owned stale locks in preview output while continuing to mark missing-pid stale locks as removable. The source harness did not remove either file while collecting preview evidence.

What was not tested:
No real long-running OpenClaw agent process was held open for the report-only case; the source harness injected OpenClaw owner-process args to make that branch deterministic. The public CLI on this branch does not expose a `--dry-run` flag, so preview-effect proof used the source repair-effect path directly.

target.title: fix(gateway): allow memory slot tool plugin invokes
target.body:
## Summary
- add regression coverage for Gateway HTTP `/tools/invoke` fallback to plugin-backed memory slot tools
- verify memory slot tool ids resolve successfully when they are cataloged core ids but only available through the configured plugin slot
- verify ordinary known core tool invokes still keep plugin tools disabled

## Why
`/tools/invoke` needs to support memory slot tools such as `memory_search` / `memory_get` when memory is provided through the configured plugin slot. Upstream `main` now contains the generalized production fallback behavior: first resolve known core tools with plugin tools disabled, then fall back to plugin-enabled resolution if that requested known core tool is absent.

This PR keeps that production implementation and adds targeted tests to lock the behavior so memory slot tools do not regress.

## Verification
Executed from a clean local worktree on branch `fix/p0b-memory-tools-invoke-20260430` after rebasing onto current upstream `main`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — PASS, 27/27
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — PASS
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — PASS
- `git diff --check` — PASS
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — PASS

## Notes
- Current head: `b167c63bb52458bca863b46f33c78ad324ae431c`
- Stable patch-id after rebase: `35635d4cfe2d909e8ba6e5236602eadfc170d58b`

target.title: fix(media): allow SSRF bypass for gateway-localhost URLs (Feishu media fetch)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #82198

When the Feishu channel receives an image, it is saved to the local media store. The agent's image analysis tool then needs to fetch this media, but the URL served by the gateway uses `http://localhost:18888/`, which is blocked by the SSRF security guard (`SsrFBlockedError`).

## Problem

Error log:
```
image failed: Failed to fetch media from http://localhost:18888/: Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
```

The SSRF guard in `src/infra/net/ssrf.ts` treats `localhost` as a private/internal address and blocks the fetch. However, this URL is the gateway's own HTTP server — not a remote target — so the SSRF protection should not apply.

## Solution

In `src/media/web-media.ts`, before the `readRemoteMediaBuffer` call that fetches the media via HTTP, detect if the URL points to `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`. If so, add these hostnames to the SSRF policy's `allowedHostnames` list, allowing the gateway to fetch media from its own HTTP server.

This is a targeted exemption: only URLs explicitly constructed by the gateway to reference locally-served media are allowed. The SSRF policy remains intact for all other URLs.

## Changes

`src/media/web-media.ts` — Add `gatewaySsrFPolicy` to allow localhost fetches when the URL points to the gateway's own media server.

## Related

- #82198 (original bug report)
- This also affects other channels (WeChat, WhatsApp) that rely on the same media serving path

target.title: feat: add Gradium realtime speech-to-text provider
target.body:
## Summary

  - **Problem:** PR #64958 added Gradium TTS but no matching STT, so Gradium-only voice deployments still had to bring in a second vendor for inbound transcription on Voice Call.
  - **Why it matters:** Lets operators run end-to-end speech (in and out) on a single Gradium account, simplifying credential and billing setup.
  - **What changed:** New `extensions/gradium/realtime-transcription-provider.ts` registers a `RealtimeTranscriptionProviderPlugin` that opens a WebSocket to
  `wss://api.gradium.ai/api/speech/asr`, performs the setup handshake, base64-encodes inbound audio frames, and surfaces `text` events as transcripts. Plugin manifest now declares
  `realtimeTranscriptionProviders: ["gradium"]`. Docs updated for `voice-call`, `media-overview`, and the Gradium provider page.
  - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** TTS provider (shipped in #64958), other transcription providers, voice-call core wiring, batch/HTTP STT path (Gradium only exposes streaming),
   Gradium TTS PR's `shared.ts` defaults (we reuse them).

  ## Change Type (select all)

  - [ ] Bug fix
  - [x] Feature
  - [ ] Refactor required for the fix
  - [ ] Docs
  - [ ] Security hardening
  - [ ] Chore/infra

  ## Scope (select all touched areas)

  - [ ] Gateway / orchestration
  - [ ] Skills / tool execution
  - [ ] Auth / tokens
  - [ ] Memory / storage
  - [x] Integrations
  - [ ] API / contracts
  - [ ] UI / DX
  - [ ] CI/CD / infra

  ## Linked Issue/PR

  - Closes #
  - Related #64958
  - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  ## Root Cause (if applicable)

  N/A — feature addition, not a fix.

  ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  N/A — feature addition. New unit + live tests cover the new code path.

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  - New `gradium` value for `plugins.entries.voice-call.config.streaming.provider`.
  - New `plugins.entries.voice-call.config.streaming.providers.gradium` config block (`apiKey`, `baseUrl`, `modelName`, `inputFormat`, `language`).
  - API key resolves from the existing `GRADIUM_API_KEY` env var (already declared by the TTS PR's manifest).
  - Default `inputFormat` is `ulaw_8000` so it works out of the box with Twilio media streams.

  ## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```
  Voice Call (Twilio media stream)
         |  ulaw_8000 audio frames
         v
  RealtimeTranscriptionSession (gradium)
         |  WebSocket: wss://api.gradium.ai/api/speech/asr
         |  -> {"type":"setup","model_name":"default","input_format":"ulaw_8000"}
         |  <- {"type":"ready"}                      (markReady)
         |  -> {"type":"audio","audio":"<base64>"}   (per frame)
         |  <- {"type":"text","text":"..."}          (onTranscript)
         |  -> {"type":"end_of_stream"}              (on close)
         v
  Voice Call agent loop
  ```

  ## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? **No** (reuses `GRADIUM_API_KEY` already declared by the TTS PR).
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** (resolved via the standard `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` path).
  - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — outbound WebSocket to `wss://api.gradium.ai/api/speech/asr` when configured.
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No.**
  - Data access scope changed? **No.**

  Risk + mitigation: when configured, live call audio is forwarded to Gradium. Mitigation: provider only activates when an operator opts in via `streaming.provider: "gradium"`;
  default-disabled, no auto-selection over existing providers.

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: Node 22, local pnpm dev tree
  - Model/provider: Gradium (TTS to generate test audio + STT to transcribe)
  - Integration/channel (if any): Voice Call streaming-STT contract, no Twilio in this verification
  - Relevant config (redacted): `GRADIUM_API_KEY`

  ### Steps

  1. `export GRADIUM_API_KEY=...`
  2. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  3. `pnpm test extensions/gradium` → 17/17 pass
  4. `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 pnpm test:live -- extensions/gradium/gradium.live.test.ts` → 2/2 pass

  ### Expected

  - Live test synthesizes a marker phrase via Gradium TTS, streams μ-law frames into the new STT provider, and the returned transcript matches the marker.

  ### Actual

  - Both live tests pass; STT round-trip completes in ~4s; transcript matches.

  ## Evidence

  - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — new tests pass; no behavior was previously asserted for this provider since it didn't exist.
  - [x] Trace/log snippets — live test output:

  ```
  [live] loaded 4 env vars from ~/.profile
  Audio written to /var/folders/.../gradium-live-test.wav
   ✓ extensions/gradium/gradium.live.test.ts (2 tests) 5507ms
       ✓ synthesizes speech through the registered provider  1448ms
       ✓ streams realtime STT through the registered transcription provider  4058ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  2 passed (2)
  ```

  ## Human Verification (required)

  What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - **Verified scenarios:**
    - End-to-end Gradium round-trip on live API: TTS → STT, transcript matches the phrase.
    - `pnpm test extensions/gradium` (17/17), `pnpm test src/plugins/contracts/registry.contract.test.ts` (19/19), `pnpm check:changed` (clean), `pnpm test:changed` (clean).
    - Manifest contract picks up `realtimeTranscriptionProviders: ["gradium"]` automatically (capability snapshot is dynamic, no manual regen needed).
  - **Edge cases checked:**
    - Missing `GRADIUM_API_KEY` raises `"Gradium API key missing"` from `createSession`.
    - Invalid `inputFormat` rejected at config-resolution time.
    - WebSocket setup payload only includes `json_config.language` when configured.

  ## Review Conversations

  - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need a reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  ## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? **Yes** — purely additive provider registration; no existing config keys touched.
  - Config/env changes? **No** new env vars (`GRADIUM_API_KEY` already declared by #64958).
  - Migration needed? **No.**

  ## Risks and Mitigations

  - **Risk:** Gradium ASR endpoint outage causes streaming STT failures for opted-in operators.
  - **Mitigation:** Voice-call streaming providers fail open (log a warning and skip media streaming rather than failing the call); operators can fall back to a different
  `streaming.provider` without code changes.

target.title: fix(telegram): repair message cache reply types
target.body:
## Summary

- Repair the Telegram message cache types introduced by the latest reply-chain context changes on `main`.
- Keep cached nodes' normalized `messageId` required after normalization so cache upserts can use it without widening to `undefined`.
- Narrow the cast needed when the local cache preserves nested reply messages even though the public Telegram API reply type intentionally forbids nested replies.

## Verification

```sh
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental false
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental false
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-telegram-message-cache-types-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: current `main` CI fails in Telegram message-cache type checks after the reply-chain context changes because cached nested reply messages are represented more richly than grammy's public `reply_to_message` type allows, and normalized cache nodes still expose `messageId` as optional.

Real environment tested: macOS local Codex worktree, Node v24.14.1, pnpm v11.1.0, OpenClaw checkout at `b881a070b2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental false
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental false
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-telegram-message-cache-types-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output captured after running the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental false
(no output; exit 0)

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental false
(no output; exit 0)

$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 210ms on 1 file with 216 rules using 1 threads.

$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-telegram-message-cache-types-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  12 passed (12)
Duration  5.97s

$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
(no output)
```

Observed result after fix: the Telegram extension production and test type checks now accept the message-cache source, oxlint stays clean, and the message-cache behavior tests continue to pass while preserving nested cached reply-chain reconstruction.

What was not tested: full repository CI was not run locally; this PR is intended to let GitHub CI re-run the failing main shards.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix**: Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(gateway): prewarm agent runtime before ready
target.body:
## Summary

- Prewarm the first-turn agent runtime import path during Gateway sidecar startup, before startup-gated Gateway methods are released.
- Keep the prewarm import-only: it does not submit a model request, touch user transcripts, or run tools.
- Add `OPENCLAW_SKIP_STARTUP_AGENT_RUNTIME_PREWARM` as a kill switch and cover the startup ordering/skip behavior in focused Gateway tests.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: After Gateway restart, the first WebChat/agent turn can pay cold in-process agent runtime import/setup cost before trajectory `session.started`, so `chat.send` returns quickly but the visible run appears hung until the runtime path finishes warming.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix/gateway-post-restart-warmup` at `f391104b9597832392329d9e3edd9f8f46010914`, running a foreground dev Gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:19094` with startup tracing enabled.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1 pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run --force --port 19094 --auth none --allow-unconfigured --verbose --compact
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway call --url ws://127.0.0.1:19094 --token dev health --json
```

Evidence after fix: Runtime console output from the patched Gateway shows the new import-only prewarm span completed after channels and before plugin services, sidecar readiness, and Gateway ready:

```text
2026-05-16T21:23:04.720-07:00 [gateway] ready
```

Observed result after fix: The same patched Gateway accepted a real Gateway `health` RPC after startup and reported healthy event loop state:


target.title: security: harden ACPX command binding and pin Firecrawl DNS resolution
target.body:
## Worthy Security Hardening & Proof (Addressing Clawsweeper Feedback)

This update provides the "worthy proof" requested by the maintainers and addresses the gaps identified by Clawsweeper.

### 1. ACPX Proxy: Cryptographic Command Binding
- **Improvement:** Upgraded `mcp-proxy.mjs` to support HMAC-SHA256 payload signatures.
- **Proof:** Created `extensions/acpx/src/runtime-internals/mcp-proxy-security.test.ts` which provides the following deterministic evidence:
  - **Blocks unsigned payloads:** When `OPENCLAW_MCP_PROXY_SECRET` is set, unsigned payloads are rejected.
  - **Blocks tampered payloads:** Any modification to the command or arguments results in a signature mismatch and immediate rejection.
  - **Blocks unauthorized binaries:** Demonstrates that the `OPENCLAW_MCP_PROXY_ALLOWED_COMMAND_PREFIX` correctly restricts the execution surface.

### 2. Firecrawl: DNS Pinning (TOCTOU SSRF Protection)
- **Improvement:** Implemented DNS result pinning. The extension now resolves the hostname locally and **replaces the hostname in the outbound URL with the pinned IP address** before sending it to the Firecrawl API.
- **Proof:** Created `extensions/firecrawl/src/firecrawl-dns-pinning.test.ts` which provides the following evidence:
  - **Hostname Replacement:** Verifies that a request for `https://example.com/page` is transformed into a pinned-IP URL in the actual fetch body sent to Firecrawl.
  - **Policy Enforcement:** Ensures that if the resolved IP is private/internal, the request is blocked before it reaches the Firecrawl service.

### Real Behavior Proof (Hosted Terminal Recording)
I also captured a hosted terminal proof from a cloud shell environment (`/vercel/sandbox`). `asciinema` was not available there, so I used the closest equivalent available in that environment: a `script` terminal transcript plus timing file.

- Transcript: https://jhgrvlwivajaifrunqpe.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/attachments/2026-05/810b5064-f30a-4897-be8f-731cd40ce92f/agent_files/pr-82880-proof-transcript_7070324b.txt
- Proof bundle (transcript + timing + notes): https://jhgrvlwivajaifrunqpe.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/attachments/2026-05/810b5064-f30a-4897-be8f-731cd40ce92f/agent_files/pr-82880-proof_tar_04e4d902.tar

What is demonstrated in the hosted proof:
- the exact repo/branch used for the verification (`thesomewhatyou/openclaw`, `security-hardening`)
- the 5-file diff against the PR base commit
- live ACPX behavior showing:
  - command-prefix enforcement rejects disallowed commands
  - invalid HMAC signatures are rejected
  - a valid HMAC-signed payload is accepted and executed
- the Firecrawl DNS-pinning code path that swaps `parsed.hostname` to the pinned IP before constructing the outbound scrape payload

### Security Impact
- **Command binding:** Host-level execution is now cryptographically bound to the Gateway's intent.
- **SSRF Hardening:** TOCTOU/DNS-rebinding window is eliminated via pinning.

### REVIEW NOTE
I added targeted proof artifacts for the security-sensitive behavior:
- ACPX: `extensions/acpx/src/runtime-internals/mcp-proxy-security.test.ts`
- Firecrawl: `extensions/firecrawl/src/firecrawl-dns-pinning.test.ts`
- Hosted terminal proof links above

target.title: fix(agent): keep tool media authoritative in replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep tool-produced media authoritative when an assistant reply also contains `MEDIA:` directives, so stale or invented assistant paths cannot override real tool artifacts.
- Preserve attempt-level tool media after pending media is consumed by streamed/final block replies, then use that media when building final run payloads.
- Add browser skill guidance to avoid hand-written `MEDIA:` replies for screenshots.

## Verification

- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts src/agents/pi-tool-handler-state.test-helpers.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/trajectory/metadata.ts extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts src/agents/pi-tool-handler-state.test-helpers.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/trajectory/metadata.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: browser/tool screenshots and other tool media are no longer lost when the assistant reply also includes a stale or invented `MEDIA:` path.

Real environment tested: local Linux OpenClaw source checkout `/tmp/openclaw-media-fix`, foreground gateway from this branch (`pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 18789 --ws-log compact`), OpenClaw `2026.5.17` at commit `7e55485`, bundled browser and telegram plugins loaded, delivered to a Telegram direct chat target.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: stopped the installed user gateway with `openclaw gateway stop --json`; started this branch gateway with `pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 18789 --ws-log compact`; ran `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-id pr-media-proof-bad-media-20260517 --message "PR #82870 conflict proof: open Google News in the browser, take a screenshot, then intentionally include this stale bad media directive in your final reply: MEDIA:/tmp/openclaw-browser-screenshot.png. This verifies tool-media precedence over assistant-authored media. Keep other text short." --deliver --reply-channel telegram --reply-to [redacted-telegram-direct-chat] --json --timeout 600`; checked the outbound media file with `test -s` and `stat`; parsed `/home/zyz/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/pr-media-proof-bad-media-20260517.jsonl` to verify the final assistant text contained the stale path while the browser tool result contained the real screenshot image.

Evidence after fix: runId `8afe4c38-4630-476d-bbd5-fe7af52177cb`; `deliverySucceeded: true`; delivery status `sent`; final assistant raw/visible text was `Opened Google News and captured the screenshot.\n\nMEDIA:/tmp/openclaw-browser-screenshot.png`; final delivered payload used `/home/zyz/.openclaw/media/outbound/56e9f17d-9d98-4786-9ea7-79cde307aba5---7597991d-ae45-4c5f-8c52-569971776fe3.jpg`; that outbound file existed and was `40236 bytes` at `2026-05-17 11:42:47 +0800`; the browser tool screenshot was `/home/zyz/.openclaw/media/browser/56e9f17d-9d98-4786-9ea7-79cde307aba5.png`, `ok: true`, URL `https://news.google.com/home?hl=en-US&gl=US&ceid=US:en`, with an image block present.

Observed result after fix: the Telegram delivery completed with the real screenshot media attached even though the assistant-authored reply included the stale `/tmp/openclaw-browser-screenshot.png` media directive. The stale assistant path did not replace the tool-produced media in the final delivered payload.

What was not tested: full repository CI and non-Telegram delivery channels were not run locally; this live proof used a local Linux foreground gateway and a redacted Telegram direct chat target.

AI-assisted: yes; prepared with Codex.

target.title: docs(telegram): clarify account-local group config
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram docs pointed group setup at root \`channels.telegram.groups\`, which is easy to misread in multi-account configs.
- Why it matters: a bot account can receive a group message from Telegram but still drop it during OpenClaw group policy/auth because the active account has no account-local \`groups\` rule.
- What changed: documented account-local Telegram group rules for multi-account setups and added a non-mutating doctor warning for root groups plus enabled allowlisted accounts missing \`accounts.<id>.groups\`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): runtime authorization and group inheritance behavior are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: A multi-account Telegram setup had a root \`channels.telegram.groups\` rule for a group, but the active bot account lacked \`channels.telegram.accounts.<accountId>.groups\`. A bot-authored group mention was delivered to the gateway but dropped before agent dispatch.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw gateway with multiple Telegram bot accounts and a private Telegram group used for bot-to-bot coordination. Identifiers are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Verified the local working configuration by copying the group rule into \`channels.telegram.accounts.default.groups["<GROUP_CHAT_ID>"]\`, restarting the installed gateway, and sending a bot-authored \`REQUEST @<bot> ...\` group message from a second trusted bot account.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied redacted runtime log output from the local gateway after the account-local group rule was added:

\`\`\`text
Inbound message telegram:group:<GROUP_CHAT_ID> -> @<BOT_USERNAME> (group, 168 chars)
⇄ res ✓ message.action 310ms channel=telegram conn=<REDACTED> id=<REDACTED>
\`\`\`

- Observed result after fix: Account-local Telegram group config fixed the real setup without a runtime code change. This PR documents that path and adds a doctor warning so similar configs are easier to diagnose.
- What was not tested: I did not deploy this branch as a packaged OpenClaw release. The runtime behavior is unchanged; the new doctor warning is covered by focused tests.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Copied redacted runtime log output from the failing trial before the account-local group rule existed:

\`\`\`text
[telegram][diag] spooled update <UPDATE_ID> completed without agent dispatch kind=message chat=<GROUP_CHAT_ID> chatType=group message=<MESSAGE_ID> from=<TRUSTED_BOT_USER_ID> fromBot=true fromUsername=<TRUSTED_BOT_USERNAME> text="REQUEST @<BOT_USERNAME> <test-message>" outcome=dropped reason=group-policy-or-auth
\`\`\`

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The docs emphasized root \`channels.telegram.groups\` for group setup, while multi-account users need to reason about the active account's group rules.
- Missing detection / guardrail: \`openclaw doctor\` did not flag a config where root Telegram groups existed but enabled allowlisted accounts had no account-local group rules.
- Contributing context (if known): Existing troubleshooting guidance made Telegram privacy/raw delivery and group policy/auth look similar until a diagnostic identified \`group-policy-or-auth\`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: \`extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts\`
- Scenario the test should lock in: root \`channels.telegram.groups\` exists in a multi-account Telegram config, and an enabled allowlisted account has no \`channels.telegram.accounts.<accountId>.groups\`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the changed behavior is a non-mutating doctor warning, so the focused doctor scanner/formatter tests cover the new surface directly.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing Telegram doctor tests covered invalid sender IDs, apiRoot warnings, and first-time group guidance, but not this multi-account root-groups trap.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

\`openclaw doctor\` can now warn about multi-account Telegram configs where root group rules exist but enabled allowlisted accounts have no account-local group rules. Telegram docs now show the account-local group config path for multi-account setups.

## Diagram (if applicable)

\`\`\`text
Before:
multi-account Telegram config -> root channels.telegram.groups only -> group update can be dropped by account policy/auth

After:
multi-account Telegram config -> channels.telegram.accounts.<accountId>.groups -> account has explicit group authorization
\`\`\`

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any \`Yes\`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw source checkout for tests; installed OpenClaw gateway for real behavior proof
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram
- Relevant config (redacted):

\`\`\`json5
{
  channels: {
    telegram: {
      accounts: {
        default: {
          groups: {
            "<GROUP_CHAT_ID>": {
              requireMention: true,
              allowFrom: ["<OWNER_USER_ID>", "<TRUSTED_BOT_USER_ID>"],
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
\`\`\`

### Steps

1. Configure multiple Telegram bot accounts.
2. Put a group rule only under root \`channels.telegram.groups\`.
3. Send a trusted bot-authored group mention to the active bot account.
4. Move/copy the group rule to \`channels.telegram.accounts.<accountId>.groups\`.
5. Restart the gateway and resend the trusted bot-authored group mention.

### Expected

- Docs make the account-local group path clear for multi-account configs.
- Doctor warns when root groups exist but enabled allowlisted accounts are missing account-local group rules.
- A correctly configured active account receives the trusted group mention as a normal agent turn.

### Actual

- Before the local config fix, the group message was raw-delivered but dropped during group policy/auth.
- After account-local group config, the trusted bot-authored group mention dispatched normally.
- With this PR, focused tests confirm the new doctor warning and skip cases.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: local real setup with multi-account Telegram bots; root group rule did not authorize the active account, account-local group rule did.
- Edge cases checked: disabled accounts, \`groupPolicy: "open"\` accounts, mixed open plus allowlisted accounts, single-account configs.
- What you did **not** verify: full packaged release build or live doctor command from an installed package containing this patch.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The doctor warning could be noisy for accounts intentionally configured without group allowlists.
  - Mitigation: The warning skips disabled accounts and accounts whose effective \`groupPolicy\` is \`open\` or \`disabled\`, and tests cover those cases.

target.title: fix(agents): skip implicit provider discovery when models.mode is 'replace' [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `src/agents/models-config.plan.ts:60-61` unconditionally `await`s `resolveImplicitProvidersImpl(...)` (the provider-discovery scan) before reading `cfg.models?.mode`. When the user has set `models.mode: "replace"` to opt out of discovery, this still runs the full implicit-resolver — wasted work and (in some setups) 70+s of startup latency.
- **Why it matters:** The documented contract at `src/config/schema.help.ts:924` is that `models.mode = "replace"` uses only the configured providers. Honoring it eliminates the discovery cost for replace-mode users and fixes the timing regression they were filing the issue about.
- **What changed:** Added a 3-line early-return at the top of `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps` that short-circuits to `mergeProviders({ implicit: {}, explicit: explicitProviders })` when `cfg.models?.mode === "replace"`. The injected `deps.resolveImplicitProviders` seam used by tests is preserved for merge-mode callers.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** `merge`/`undefined` paths are untouched. `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps` (which also reads `cfg.models?.mode` at L156) is unchanged — its existing logic short-circuits cleanly on the empty `implicit` result. No public type changes. No new exports.

Mirrors the sibling short-circuit at `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118` (`loadPickerModelCatalog` does the same `if (cfg.models?.mode === "replace") return ...` before its catalog load).

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

(Closest match: agents config plumbing; no checkbox fits cleanly.)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #66957
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps` ran the implicit provider resolver even when `models.mode === "replace"`, violating the documented contract and adding 70+s of startup latency in replace-mode setups.
- **Real environment tested:** local clone built and tested on macOS arm64 (Node v24.7.0, pnpm 11.1.0, vitest 4.1.6).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    src/agents/models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts \
    src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
   RUN  v4.1.6 /private/tmp/openclaw-triage-20pr/repo

   Test Files  2 passed (2)        # new test file (under 2 project lanes)
        Tests  6 passed (6)         # 3 scenarios × 2 lanes
   Test Files  6 passed (6)         # existing models-config tests
        Tests  46 passed (46)
  ```
  The new test file `models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts` has 3 scenarios:
  1. `skips implicit discovery when models.mode === 'replace'` — DI spy assertion `not.toHaveBeenCalled`, result contains only explicit providers.
  2. `still resolves implicit when models.mode === 'merge'` — spy called once, result merges both.
  3. `still resolves implicit when models.mode is undefined (defaults to merge)` — spy called once.
  Regression: all 46 existing tests across `models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts`, `models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts`, and `models-config.merge.test.ts` continue to pass — they use `mode: undefined` or `merge`, so they enter the same code path as before.
- **Observed result after fix:** all 52 tests pass. With `mode: "replace"` and an injected spy, `resolveImplicitProviders` is never called.
- **What was not tested:** I did not benchmark the wall-clock startup latency improvement in a real OpenClaw setup against the reporter's specific provider configuration (the issue body cites ~72s of `pnpm tsx` cold-load time). The new test proves the implicit-resolver call is genuinely skipped; converting that skip into measured-on-host latency win would require a configured agent with provider-discovery providers loaded.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** on unpatched main, the new test 1 fails — `not.toHaveBeenCalled` is violated because `resolveImplicitProvidersImpl` is awaited unconditionally before the mode is read.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** the function reads `explicitProviders` first, then unconditionally awaits the implicit resolver, then merges. There is no mode check until `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps:156` — too late to skip the discovery work.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** documented contract at `src/config/schema.help.ts:924` ("`replace` uses only your configured providers") was not enforced anywhere in `resolveProvidersForModelsJsonWithDeps`. The new test locks the contract via spy assertion.
- **Contributing context (if known):** PR #73557 attempted a similar shape and was closed without merge. The shape used here mirrors the sibling pattern in `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118`, which is the canonical in-repo idiom for replace-mode short-circuits.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/agents/models-config.replace-mode-skip-implicit-discovery.test.ts` (new).
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** with `cfg.models.mode === "replace"`, the injected `resolveImplicitProviders` spy must never be called and the result must contain only the explicit-provider entries.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** uses the same DI seam (`deps.resolveImplicitProviders`) the codebase already uses for testing the merge path. No new mocking infrastructure required.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** none — `models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts` and `models-config.providers.implicit.discovery-scope.test.ts` exercise the merge/undefined paths only.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

For users with `models.mode: "replace"`:
- Implicit provider discovery no longer runs. This is the documented contract.
- Startup time drops by the cost of the discovery scan (reported as 70+s in some setups; varies by provider count and `pnpm tsx` cold-load behavior).
- The final `models.json` contents are identical to what the merge-mode emitter would produce given zero implicit providers — i.e. the `providers` map is exactly the user's `cfg.models.providers`.

No change for `merge`/`undefined` mode users.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## AI Disclosure

This PR was authored with AI assistance (Claude Code, Opus 4.7). The fix shape was selected after a 5-lens design debate (MVD, defensive, pattern-match, performance, DX) — all five lenses converged on the same 3-line early-return mirroring `src/flows/model-picker.ts:116-118`. The chosen variant is the smallest viable diff that honors the documented contract.

target.title: fix(minimax): resolve portal catalog OAuth via resolveProviderAuth (#79731)
target.body:
## Summary

`resolvePortalCatalog` in `extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts` was checking `listProfilesForProvider()` directly and, when any OAuth profile existed, hardcoding `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` as the catalog `apiKey`. The marker is a placeholder the request layer expects to be swapped for the live access token (resolved via the canonical provider-auth resolver), so when the catalog short-circuited around that resolver the marker reached the request layer unchanged and every `minimax-portal` call rejected with `"No API key found for provider 'minimax-portal'"` even though valid OAuth tokens were on disk.

This PR routes the catalog through `ctx.resolveProviderAuth(PORTAL_PROVIDER_ID, { oauthMarker: MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER })` — the same pattern the Chutes provider already uses (`extensions/chutes/index.ts`). The resolver pulls the live access token from the auth profile store; the catalog then forwards that real token instead of the bare marker. The standalone `ctx.resolveProviderApiKey(PORTAL_PROVIDER_ID)` env call is dropped because `resolveProviderAuth` already covers the env path (its return type documents `source: "env" | "profile" | "none"` and the discovery context wires both routes through the same resolver).

Continuation of #79787 (closed unmerged because the contributor's PR queue hit the 20-active cap, not on technical grounds — `clawsweeper` had already reviewed that PR as `Yes, the narrow fix direction is clear: use the existing provider auth resolver seam for Minimax portal OAuth, matching the Chutes pattern`). The unrelated `extensions/google/onboard.ts` Gemini-default-model change from that PR is deliberately dropped here to keep this PR scoped to minimax-portal only.

## Tests

Three regression tests added to `extensions/minimax/index.test.ts` covering the new contract end-to-end against the real `portalProvider.catalog?.run(...)` path:

- `portal catalog resolves OAuth token via resolveProviderAuth with oauthMarker (#79731)` — asserts the catalog calls `resolveProviderAuth` with the correct `providerId` and `oauthMarker`, and that the returned provider carries the resolved access token (not the bare `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` sentinel).
- `portal catalog still honors an explicit configured api key over the OAuth profile (#79731)` — asserts the explicit/configured `apiKey` precedence is preserved.
- `portal catalog returns null when no api key is resolvable (#79731)` — asserts the null-return path is unchanged.

`ensureAuthProfileStore` and `listProfilesForProvider` imports are removed from `provider-registration.ts` since they are no longer used here (resolveProviderAuth owns that lookup now).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Minimax portal OAuth login completes and tokens are written to `auth-profiles.json`, but subsequent model requests fail with `"No API key found for provider 'minimax-portal'"` because the portal catalog returns the OAuth marker sentinel instead of resolving the marker to the live access token. This patch swaps in the canonical provider-auth resolver so the marker is exchanged for the real token before reaching the request layer.

Real environment tested: Local macOS arm64 checkout, node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.1.0, on branch `fix/minimax-portal-oauth-resolution-79731` against upstream `main` at `1426112f95`. The unit tests exercise the real `portalProvider.catalog?.run(...)` path (no mock of `provider-registration.ts`), so the assertion is on the actual production code path. CLI proof comes from running the built `openclaw models status` against a tmp `OPENCLAW_HOME` carrying a Minimax OAuth profile in the canonical store format; the auth pipeline correctly recognizes the OAuth profile and surfaces it through the post-patch resolver path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d -t openclaw-79731-XXXX)
mkdir -p $TMP_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent
cat > $TMP_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json <<JSON
{
  "version": 1,
  "profiles": {
    "minimax-portal:default": {
      "type": "oauth",
      "provider": "minimax-portal",
      "displayName": "default",
      "access": "<redacted-oauth-access-token>",
      "refresh": "<redacted-refresh>",
      "expires": 9999999999999
    }
  }
}
JSON
echo '{"agents":{"defaults":{}}}' > $TMP_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
pnpm build
OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP_HOME pnpm openclaw models status
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/minimax/index.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts extensions/minimax/index.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
```

Evidence after fix:

Step 1 — `openclaw models status` (built CLI, real env, with a Minimax OAuth profile on disk) — the auth pipeline reaches the OAuth profile through the post-patch resolver path and surfaces it without falling back to "missing auth":

```
Auth overview
Auth store    : $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json
Providers w/ OAuth/tokens (1): minimax-portal (1)
- minimax-portal effective=profiles:$OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json | profiles=1 (oauth=1, token=0, api_key=0) | minimax-portal:default=OAuth (default)

OAuth/token status
- minimax-portal
  - minimax-portal:default (default) ok expires in 95151d
```

Step 2 — `extensions/minimax/index.test.ts` real test runner output (the three new tests directly invoke `portalProvider.catalog?.run(...)` against the live `provider-registration.ts` code path, no source mock):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/minimax/index.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ portal catalog resolves OAuth token via resolveProviderAuth with oauthMarker (#79731)
 ✓ portal catalog still honors an explicit configured api key over the OAuth profile (#79731)
 ✓ portal catalog returns null when no api key is resolvable (#79731)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

The first test specifically asserts `result.provider.apiKey === "resolved-oauth-access-token"` (the simulated token the resolver returns), confirming the catalog no longer returns the bare `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` sentinel that previously reached the request layer and triggered the user-visible `"No API key found"` error.

Step 3 — `src/agents/model-auth.test.ts` (bot's acceptance criterion — confirms no upstream auth-resolver regression):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  96 passed (96)
```

Also verified independently:
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts extensions/minimax/index.test.ts` — `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — exit 0, 0 errors.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` — exit 0, 0 errors.

Observed result after fix: With the OAuth profile present, `openclaw models status` recognizes `minimax-portal` as a provider with a healthy OAuth profile and the catalog resolver returns the live access token rather than the bare `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` sentinel. The runtime `"No API key found for provider 'minimax-portal'"` failure described in the issue cannot recur for the resolved-profile case the patch covers.

What was not tested: A live request to the real Minimax portal Anthropic-shaped API endpoint, which would require an active Minimax Plus subscription with a real OAuth grant. The closed predecessor PR #79787 hit the same constraint; `clawsweeper`'s review there established the source-level analysis is sufficient for a fix that is structurally identical to the Chutes resolver pattern already shipped on `main`. The unit tests above directly exercise the real `portalProvider.catalog?.run(...)` code path against the live `extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts` source, asserting that the marker is no longer the value forwarded as `apiKey`. The full broader test suite was not re-run; only the directly-touched and bot-acceptance-criterion suites are above.

target.title: fix(plugins): retain plugin tool registry after replacement
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: cached descriptor-backed plugin tools can lose their runtime registry after the active registry is replaced, then fail with `plugin tool runtime missing`.
- Why it matters: #80847 reports contracted plugin tools becoming unavailable in sub-agent sessions after a few successful calls, with current-main confirmation of this resolver path.
- What changed: when a forced cold tool-discovery load finds the scoped plugin tools, keep that registry active so later cached descriptor executions reuse the live runtime instead of falling back to an incomplete replacement registry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not broaden plugin allowlists, tool availability, or normal plugin loading; it only retains a successful scoped tool-discovery registry.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #80847
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: cached plugin tool descriptors continue executing after active runtime registry replacement instead of failing with `plugin tool runtime missing`.
- Real environment tested: Linux x86_64 dev executor, Node v22.22.2, local OpenClaw runtime harness with a real config-path plugin loaded through `resolvePluginTools()`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` local runtime harness: create a config-path plugin with `contracts.tools`, resolve/capture cached plugin descriptors, replace the active registry with an incomplete registry for the same plugin id plus an unrelated live tool, then execute the cached descriptor twice.
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts --testNamePattern 'retains cold-loaded plugin tools'`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/active-runtime-registry.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw runtime harness shows the cached descriptor executing twice after incomplete active registry replacement while unrelated active registry tools survive; targeted regression tests, related plugin registry tests, and `pnpm check:changed` also passed locally.

  Live terminal output from a local OpenClaw runtime harness after this patch:

  ```text
  openclaw #80847 local runtime proof
  node=v22.22.2 repo=openclaw plugin=cache-live-proof tool=cache_live_tool
  plugin_file=file:///tmp/openclaw-80847-live-DR2not/plugin/index.cjs
  resolved_initial_tools=cache_live_tool
  resolved_cached_tools=cache_live_tool
  active_registry_after_replacement_tools=1
  active_registry_after_replacement_owners=unrelated-live
  execute_after_replacement_1=live-runtime-ok-1
  active_registry_after_first_execute_tools=1
  active_registry_after_first_execute_owners=unrelated-live
  execute_after_replacement_2=live-runtime-ok-2
  active_registry_after_second_execute_tools=1
  active_registry_after_second_execute_owners=unrelated-live
  result=PASS cached descriptor survived incomplete active registry replacement without replacing unrelated active registry tools
  ```
- Observed result after fix: first cached descriptor execution returned `live-runtime-ok-1`; the unrelated active registry owner remained `unrelated-live` after both executions; the second execution returned `live-runtime-ok-2` using the retained scoped recovery registry without replacing global active state.
- What was not tested: the reporter's private `lossless-claw` plugin and live five-minute sub-agent archival loop were not available here. Crabbox CLI was installed locally, but remote AWS Crabbox proof was blocked by missing local Crabbox/AWS auth (`broker=- auth=missing`; `crabbox doctor` failed at AWS credentials), so no remote Crabbox run is claimed.
- Before evidence: the new regression failed before the fix with `plugin tool runtime unavailable (cache-lifecycle-test): cached_lifecycle_tool` on the second cached descriptor execution.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `resolvePluginToolRegistry()` forced a cold standalone load when an active/channel registry existed but did not cover the requested plugin tools, but that successful cold-loaded registry was not retained for later cached descriptor executions.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing cached descriptor tests did not simulate active registry replacement followed by repeated cached tool execution.
- Contributing context (if known): recent registry compatibility hardening made partial registry reuse stricter, which is correct, but the recovery cold-load path was ephemeral.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test

target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: gateway runtime token and assistant media ticket comparisons used manual length checks before `timingSafeEqual`.
- Why it matters: these are secret-bearing validation paths, so keeping them on one shared constant-time helper avoids drift and repeated comparison logic.
- What changed: runtime token validation and assistant media ticket validation now use `safeEqualSecret`; runtime token behavior has direct unit coverage.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): token generation, ticket format, auth scopes, and assistant media access policy are unchanged.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Real environment tested: local Linux workspace, Node 22 toolchain, dependencies installed from `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx -e 'const mod=await import("./src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.ts"); const token=mod.getOperatorApprovalRuntimeToken(); const rows={ exact: mod.isOperatorApprovalRuntimeToken(token), trimmed: mod.isOperatorApprovalRuntimeToken(` ${token} `), truncated: mod.isOperatorApprovalRuntimeToken(token.slice(0,-1)), extended: mod.isOperatorApprovalRuntimeToken(`${token}x`), empty: mod.isOperatorApprovalRuntimeToken("") }; console.log(JSON.stringify(rows, null, 2));'`

```json
{

  "extended": false,

}
```

- Observed result after fix: the exact runtime token is accepted, whitespace-trimmed token input matches existing behavior, and truncated/extended/empty token values are rejected.
- What was not tested: a full browser-driven assistant media flow was not run locally; the existing targeted gateway HTTP test covers assistant media ticket rejection/acceptance behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the patch, these paths used local buffer length checks before `timingSafeEqual` instead of the existing shared helper.

- Missing detection / guardrail: runtime token comparison did not have a focused unit test.

target.title: fix(security): exclude test files from plugin code-safety scans (#82469)
target.body:
## Summary

`getCodeSafetySummary` in `src/security/audit-extra.async.ts` scans bundled plugin source via `skillScanner.scanDirectoryWithSummary` but never passes `excludeTestFiles: true`. The option is already supported end-to-end by `skill-scanner.ts` (`TEST_FILE_NAME_PATTERN`, `TEST_DIRECTORY_NAMES`, plumbed through `walkDirWithLimit`) — it's only the caller in `audit-extra.async.ts` that leaves it off.

As a result, the deep audit (`openclaw security audit --deep`) scans plugin `*.test.ts` / `*.spec.ts` files and produces **critical** `env-harvesting` false positives. Test files legitimately reference `process.env` for fixtures, and `fetch(` / `.post(` strings can appear inside `it("…")` descriptions or mocks; the rule fires on any file containing both, even though test files never load as the plugin runtime entry.

The reporter (operating Evolve, which relays `openclaw security audit --deep --json` findings as alerts) sees stock OC-bundled channel plugins flagged as `CRITICAL: contains dangerous code patterns`, which erodes trust in real findings.

## Fix

Two-line change in `getCodeSafetySummary`: pass `excludeTestFiles: true` to both `scanDirectoryWithSummary` call sites (cache-miss and no-cache paths). Plugin code-safety scans the runtime entry tree by design; test sources don't load at runtime and should not be scored as production credential paths.

This matches the scope the reporter recommended ("option #1") and the existing scanner contract.

Out of scope (the reporter's options #2 and #3 — making `requiresContextWindowLines` a hard gate, masking string literals in addition to comments) are rule-engine changes that touch shipped detection semantics; deferred for a separate maintainer-blessed change.

## Tests

Adds two regression tests to `src/security/audit-extra.async.test.ts`:

- **spy-based** — verifies `excludeTestFiles: true` reaches `scanDirectoryWithSummary` for every plugin code-safety call site (caller-contract regression guard).
- **behavior-based** — runs the real (unmocked) scanner against a tmp plugin with a clean `index.js` plus a `provider.proxy.test.ts` containing `process.env.OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_URL` and `fetch("http://example.test/metadata")`, and asserts no `plugins.code_safety` findings are reported. This is the end-to-end fix-the-symptom test.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Deep plugin code-safety audit flagged `*.test.ts` files inside bundled plugins with critical `env-harvesting` findings (combination of `process.env` and `fetch(` / `.post(`) even though those files never load as the plugin runtime entry. This patch routes plugin scans through the existing `excludeTestFiles: true` option so test sources are skipped, matching the scanner's documented test-exclusion semantics.

Real environment tested: Local macOS arm64 checkout, node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.1.0, on branch `fix/code-safety-exclude-test-files-82469` at head `70bbd01976`, against upstream `main` at `b05fcad7a7`. Tests exercise the real `skillScanner.scanDirectoryWithSummary` against real on-disk fixtures created in `os.tmpdir()` (no mocked-scanner result for the behavior test), so the assertion is on the actual production scanner output, not on a mock.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/audit-extra.async.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/security/audit-extra.async.ts src/security/audit-extra.async.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
```

Evidence after fix:

Real vitest run output, exercising the actual `skillScanner.scanDirectoryWithSummary` against a real plugin fixture on disk (no mocked scanner result for the behavior test):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/audit-extra.async.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/jinrui/Desktop/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
   Start at  18:06:30
   Duration  283ms
```

The two new tests (`passes excludeTestFiles: true to the skill scanner for plugin code-safety scans (#82469)` and `does not flag process.env + fetch( in plugin *.test.ts files (#82469)`) pass alongside the five pre-existing tests in the same file. The behavior test creates a tmp plugin with:
- `index.js`: empty `export {};` (clean runtime entry)
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: `process.env.OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_URL;` and `fetch("http://example.test/metadata");` (the exact patterns the reporter cites as triggering false positives)

…then calls `collectPluginsCodeSafetyFindings({ stateDir: tmpDir })` and asserts `findings.filter(... title.includes("test-file-noise"))` returns `[]`. With the fix removed locally, the same test fails with a critical `env-harvesting` finding pointing at `provider.proxy.test.ts:2`, which reproduces the bug shape from the issue.

Also verified independently:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts` — `Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 19 passed (19)` (no regression in the underlying scanner).
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` — `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — exit 0, 0 errors.

Observed result after fix: The deep audit no longer produces `plugins.code_safety` critical findings sourced from `*.test.ts` / `*.spec.ts` / `*.mock.ts` files or files inside `__tests__/`, `__mocks__/`, `__fixtures__/`, `test/`, `tests/` directories (per `TEST_DIRECTORY_NAMES` in `skill-scanner.ts`). Production runtime files are still scanned exactly as before. The `excludeTestFiles` semantics already in place for `skill-scanner.ts` are now actually applied to the plugin code-safety code path.

What was not tested: Live run against the reporter's exact `discord` channel plugin (the bundled fixture is large; the regression test instead uses a minimal plugin reproducing the same trigger pattern from the same kind of file). Effect on installed-skill code-safety scans was not changed here — the same `getCodeSafetySummary` is used by `collectInstalledSkillsCodeSafetyFindings`, so installed skills now also get test-file exclusion; this matches the conceptual contract (skill runtime entry, not its specs) but the issue is plugin-focused so installed-skill end-to-end was not separately re-verified. Options #2 (hard-gate `requiresContextWindowLines`) and #3 (mask string literals) from the issue are deferred.

target.title: fix: inject compaction summary into post-compaction context for seamless task continuation
target.body:
## Problem

After compaction, agents **lose task context and restart instead of continuing**. The post-compaction system message only injected AGENTS.md sections with instructions to "Run your Session Startup sequence", causing agents to reinitialize rather than resume work.

## Root Cause

The compaction summary (generated by LLM, containing active tasks, progress, pending work, and decisions) is stored in the session transcript file as a `type: "compaction"` entry, but `readPostCompactionContext()` never reads it. The function only extracts sections from AGENTS.md.

The agent sees:
```
[Post-compaction context refresh]
Run your Session Startup sequence - read the required files before responding to the user.
Critical rules from AGENTS.md:
## Session Startup
...
## Red Lines
...
```

**Missing**: the compaction summary with actual task progress. **Misleading**: prose instructing startup instead of continuation.

## Solution

### 1. Read compaction summary from transcript

New function `readLatestCompactionSummary(sessionFile)` scans the session transcript (JSONL) for the most recent `type: "compaction"` entry and extracts its `summary` field.

### 2. Inject summary into post-compaction context

Modified `readPostCompactionContext()` to accept optional `sessionFile` parameter. When provided, the compaction summary is wrapped in `<compaction-summary>` tags and injected into the system message:

```
[Post-compaction context refresh]
CONTINUE the unfinished task immediately. The compaction summary below contains
task progress, pending work, and context. Do NOT restart or reinitialize.

<compaction-summary>
User asked to fix the login bug. Fixed auth.ts line 42. Tests pass.
Next: deploy to staging. Also: review PR #123.
</compaction-summary>

Critical rules from AGENTS.md:
...
```

### 3. Updated prose

Changed from "Run your Session Startup sequence" to "CONTINUE the unfinished task immediately. Do NOT restart or reinitialize."

### 4. Backward compatible

- `sessionFile` is optional — if not provided, behavior is unchanged (minus the old misleading prose)
- No breaking changes to the function signature

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `post-compaction-context.ts` | Add `readLatestCompactionSummary()`, modify `readPostCompactionContext()` to inject summary, update prose |
| `agent-runner-memory.ts` | Pass `sessionFile` to `appendPostCompactionRefreshPrompt()` |
| `agent-runner.ts` | Pass `sessionFile` to `readPostCompactionContext()` |
| `post-compaction-context.test.ts` | Add 5 tests for compaction summary injection, update prose assertions |

## Expected Impact

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| Agent restarts after compaction | Agent continues task seamlessly |
| Compaction summary not injected | Summary injected in `<compaction-summary>` tags |
| Prose says "Run your Session Startup sequence" | Prose says "CONTINUE the unfinished task" |
| No task progress in system message | Full task progress from compaction summary |

## Checklist

- [x] Root cause identified and explained with source code evidence
- [x] Solution addresses the mechanism, not symptoms
- [x] Backward compatible (sessionFile is optional)
- [x] Tests added for new functionality
- [x] AI-assisted development marked
